article_meta.title: Federal immigration officers board an elevator with respondents at immigration court in New York on 17 November 2025

reasoning: The summary avoids most of the Guardian’s loaded language (e.g., replaces “spied” with “conducted surveillance”) and attributes critical characterizations to named critics. One mildly loaded term (“historical FBI abuses”) appears in the model’s own voice, and the summary slants toward critics by omitting the DOJ/NYPD defenses included in the article.

custom_reasoning.spin: Language is mostly neutral; “surveillance” and attributions are used. One loaded noun (“abuses”) appears.
custom_reasoning.unsubstantiated_claims: Claims about legality/non-violence reflect article statements and are broadly factual; no new unsupported claims.
custom_reasoning.opinion_as_fact: Minimal; evaluative statements are largely attributed. “Abuses” is the main exception.
custom_reasoning.sensationalism: No dramatic or alarming phrasing beyond factual description.
custom_reasoning.mudslinging: No personal attacks in the model’s voice.
custom_reasoning.mind_reading: No attributions of motives without evidence.
custom_reasoning.slant: Emphasizes critics and civil liberties concerns; underplays law enforcement/DOJ framing.
custom_reasoning.bias_by_omission: Omits DOJ’s pro-enforcement statement and much of NYPD’s justification/oversight details present in the article.
custom_reasoning.word_choice: Avoids politically charged terms from the source (e.g., “spied,” “regime”); mostly neutral choices.
custom_reasoning.negativity_bias: Focuses on surveillance concerns and historical comparisons; less on official justifications.
custom_reasoning.elite_populist_bias: No systematic favoring of elites vs grassroots; cites both officials and advocates.
custom_reasoning.framing_neutrality: More neutral than the source overall but includes a small amount of loaded framing and omissions.
custom_reasoning.perspective_completeness: Does not include key law enforcement/DOJ rationales that appear in the article; sources list is broad but summary omits that viewpoint.
